title: Statsig Event
description: An event object used for logging exposure events, custom events, and webhook payloads in the Statsig analytics pipeline. Events power experiment analysis, product analytics, and metric computations.

eventName.description: The name of the event. Exposure events use the format statsig::gate_exposure, statsig::config_exposure, or statsig::experiment_exposure. Config change events use statsig::config_change. Custom events use application-defined names.

time.description: Timestamp of the event in milliseconds since epoch.

value.description: An optional value associated with the event, such as a revenue amount, duration, or string label.

metadata.description: Optional metadata key-value pairs providing additional context for the event.

gate.description: The name of the gate, for gate exposure events.

config.description: The name of the config or experiment, for config or experiment exposure events.

ruleID.description: The rule that matched during evaluation.

type.description: The entity type for config_change events (e.g., Gate, Experiment, DynamicConfig).

name.description: The entity name for config_change events.

action.description: The action performed for config_change events (e.g., created, updated, deleted).

timeUUID.description: A unique identifier for this event instance, combining timestamp and random components.

StatsigUser.description: The user object representing the end user associated with the event.

userID.description: A unique identifier for the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

name.description: The display name of the user.

ip.description: The IP address of the user.

country.description: The two-letter country code of the user.

locale.description: The locale identifier for the user.

appVersion.description: The version of the application the user is running.

custom.description: Custom user properties used for targeting rules.

privateAttributes.description: Private user attributes used for evaluation but not logged to Statsig servers.

customIDs.description: Custom identifier mappings such as companyID, teamID, or other organizational units.

StatsigMetadata.description: Metadata about the SDK that generated the event.

sdkType.description: The type of SDK (e.g., js-client, py-server, react-native, node-server).

sessionID.description: The session identifier for client-side SDKs.

stableID.description: A stable device identifier for anonymous user tracking.
